_comment: GYF model registry — the machine-enforced license/lane gate (engineering-doctrine D2). Every model GYF can load is tagged here. CI (scripts/check_model_licenses.py) fails if any 'production' model is not commercial-clean + evaluated. RESEARCH-lane models are OFFLINE-only north-stars and never served. Keep in lockstep with tech-stack.md.

models.name: marqo-fashionSigLIP
models.notes: Shipped P1-A perception; superseded by google-siglip2-base at M2 (lost the bake-off on mrr/recall@5/recall@10). Kept as the commercial-clean fallback baseline behind the encoder port (invariant #5).

models.name: google-siglip2-base
models.notes: Promoted at M2. SigLIP 2 recipe, B/16, 768-dim (same width as the old incumbent => no vector migration). Won the M2 bake-off against marqo-fashionSigLIP on mrr/recall@5/recall@10; despite not being fashion-tuned, the SigLIP 2 training recipe wins. Served via local SiglipEncoder baseline or the ZeroGPU remote lane.

models.name: google-siglip2-so400m
models.notes: M2 bake-off candidate. Larger backbone (1152-dim → needs a vector-dim migration + re-embed if promoted). Only escalate to this if the base candidate is competitive. Research-lane until proven.

models.name: sam-3d-body
models.notes: Body-type estimator (M3, plan p1b-cycle2). Promote to production once M3 ships + eval attached + SAM License accepted.

models.name: mhr
models.notes: Parametric body model SAM 3D Body decodes into; SMPL-free. Promote with M3.

models.name: anny
models.train_data_license: Apache-2.0 / CC0 (MakeHuman); avoid the SMPL-X topology variant
models.notes: WHO-calibrated body model for measurement-threshold calibration + fairness (M3).

models.name: qwen3
models.notes: LLM upgrade for the NL goal box (currently rule-based parse_goal). Promote when wired behind IntentParser port.

models.name: fitdit
models.notes: North-star try-on quality, OFFLINE benchmark only. MUST NOT be served — see engineering-doctrine try-on path (use a licensed model at inference).

models.name: idm-vton
models.notes: Non-commercial try-on baseline; research-lane reference only.

models.name: fashn-tryon-v1.6
models.license: Proprietary (hosted API; ToS grants commercial use of outputs)
models.train_data_license: Vendor-proprietary (licensed model at inference — doctrine D2 beta lane; no weights ever touch GYF infra)
models.notes: M9 beta try-on lane behind the TryOnRenderer port (licensed hosted model at inference — doctrine D2; no weights ever touch GYF infra; 72h vendor auto-delete + return_base64 keeps generated user imagery off vendor storage, D8). One garment per call — the adapter composes top→bottom sequentially; footwear unsupported and honestly skipped. PROMOTION (D5): buy API credits, render the eval look-set, record eval-reports/tryon-fashn-v1.json, flip lane to production, then set GYF_TRYON_PROVIDER=fashn + GYF_FASHN_API_KEY. Until then the NullTryOnRenderer baseline serves (abstains honestly). Own-it-later: permissive architecture trained on brand on-model photos behind the same port.
